summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall covertly exfiltrates developer identity (git email, GitHub login, hostname) to a third-party Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests developer identity data far beyond what the README advertises as 'anonymous' telemetry. It reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract the installer's git author email and GitHub CLI username/email, then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), platform, Node version, and CI environment indicators. The combined payload is POSTed as JSON via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ — a generic Google Cloud Run URL unrelated to the package's stated homepage (ai-sdk.guide), so it is not a publisher-documented telemetry endpoint. Reading ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml at install time additionally touches the GitHub CLI's authenticated-host file, which commonly contains an oauth_token, even though the current parser only extracts identity fields. The README's 'anonymous (platform, Node version, architecture)' claim directly contradicts the code, which collects PII (developer email, GitHub login, hostname, username, working directory). This is covert identity harvesting disguised as telemetry, fired automatically at install with no opt-out.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
